author: @dimensionprint5096

author: @hatlangston1379
text: I have always wanted to try a weight on a lever as a gravity battery.  The lever could apply amazing torque.  It might be simpler than chains or belts.  Anyway,  I always find your vids very interesting.

author: @SkyNetworkSolutions
text: great vid.try making a low friction gear box.

Well as long as you have water this type of thing with buckets will work as generator without any special parts. Someone is even trying the same but inverse with bubbles that get trapped when water falls down a hole, which can also pressurize the air. Tho because the belt has to flex all the time it probably cant last very long before it wears out

author: @JakobDraeby

author: @X02switchblades
text: As I was walking yesterday I've noticed a guy holding a pedestal fan and going his own way. There was a light breeze and the prop was spinning!!! Could an ordinary fan work as a wind generator? Their broad props should capture much of the wind I think

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: if you can get it to heat weld together 2liter PET material seems like a good choice for the belt, just look up lace cutters that turn say a disc of leather into one long strip by cutting a hole in the middle and starting to cut it from there with a blade that has a stop so it can't cut through more of the leather at once than the depth of that gap behind the blade, so something similar to that to cut a bottle into a long spiral might be just what is needed there, or that concept of the double sided like an 8 that they pass each link of the leather through to make belts and such maybe, but out of that PET since it does not stretch much, and is incredibly durable and resistant (left a couple in Oklahoma summer sun directly for nearly 5 years and not a bit of yellow, no frosted effect of oxidation ect, and just as flexible to my human perception at least as the day it was left out there, I am currently looking into a design to use it for protecting solar cells in a solar panel in lu of using some sort of tempered glass, (also going to test gorrilla glue crystal version that says leaving the bottle in the dark could cause it to turn yellow, so leave it out in the light to prevent yellowing, as a method of sealing the cells from water and oxygen damage too, just hope it can last in sun, and does not shrink causing the cells to flex and snap of course, why? because the silicon sealant stuff you buy for solar panels is like 80 bucks here for two quart containers of the stuff LOL.

author: @Ecclesiasticus
text:
The hardest part im running into with generators is energy storage. Every method generates different power - and not being a rocket scientist i dont know how to shove that power into a battery.
Buying a charger circuit feels like cheating.

author: @clivetaylor27
text: Robert, I did scan your work, have you any ideas for a flywheel generator. As lithium becomes a commodity (exploited) EV's might practically switch out the electrical batteries for flywheels (or air of course). I'm sure you're aware of the science and history. Do you think they could become a practical or viable home solution for EV's to reduce costs and charging times, lifetimes etc? And commercialisable? And then automatically ready for domestic power storage.

author: @wardp.9832
text: So Same design - BUT under a RAIN GUTTER; CUPS on the Belt to catch water; And a BELL SYPHON in the gutter to DUMP a FULL CUP amount of WATER down onto the Belt/Cup. Should be pretty efficient too with some timing tweaks.  Will work in both a slow or fast rainfall.   If done well it would probably look really neat too

author: @jamesross1003
text: High ratio gear box.  The friction of the gear box would slow down the dropping weight while at the same time speed up the generator.  Could even be done with a lego gear set.

replies.author: @jamesross1003
replies.text: Also, since the higher the load on the circuit connected to the generator slows down the generator it would need to be taken into consideration.  Math<-----got to do it

text: If you put a 3-4W Microwave Synchronous motor on it, the gearing would slow the drop and produce 240v - 3-4w and for a longer time.

author: @ChadKovac
text: Have you done or would you please do a tour of your most used tools in the shop?

author: @weslingm
text:
I have given this alot of thought. 
You need a 500 lb. weight, a chain hoist and a structure outside.30 foot drop on the weight, gear reduction and a generator for your house. Probably a battery bank to store the energy. Probably a young mans game! But with the right gear reduction I think it would power you house better than solar or a wind system. And could easily supplement either system.

author: @HIVELESSB
text: Idea for improvements. Gears to slow the wheel + Ram pump for pertpetual water wheel. ;)

author: @lowrads
text: Correct me if I'm wrong, but isn't the energy of a liter of petrol the same as the potential energy of an entire loaded lorry, ~44 tonnes, suspended at 100m?  Really makes you appreciate energy and how we misuse it.

author: @devinkrike5968
text: that thing needs need a lot more gearing. That way it takes the weight longer to hit the floor. Perhaps take gearbox from another drill(provided the drill doesn't have braking.) and remove the motor and connect it to the chuck of the generator. That will gear it up even more and pehaps it will slow down the descent while providing 2x the rotation speed for the motor.

author: @mikeyearwood
text: Can't every elevator be a gravity generator?

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Great fun. Wouldn't it be good if you could have a weight on each side of the belt and somehow got it to go up and down continually, much like a pendulum (as you said) going side to side. Not sure if you could power a pump to use water and still have power left over to power the light.

author: @jeffreyrood8755
text:
Great video. Failing is important too. That way we know what won't work.
I had a quick thought, is there a way to use the freewheel sprockets and a weighty flywheel and when the weight hits bottom, it free spins from the weighted flywheel?

author: @kilokilos
text: The light house at Cape Agulhas, if I remember, originally had a clas1 Fresnel lenses weighing about 3 tons. It floated in a bathing of mercury and was turned by using a bucket of sand the lighthouse keeper wound up the light house on a rope and using it as a gravity battery to rotate the mechanism. It rotated to give a flash of light every 5 seconds that could be seen 32 km away. Not bad for a 171 year old gravity battery. The light was fed by burning whale oil.

replies.author: @justinw1765
replies.text: Pretty cool minus the whale oil.

author: @neatt3815
text: I love gravity generators 😁

author: @ianfisher6561

author: @mikebond6328
text: I’ve been thinking for a while about designing a gravity battery using water as the ballast. A small solar powered pump could be used to pump the water back to the top during the day.

replies.author: @lowrads
replies.text: The nice thing is that it uses no exotic materials.  The unfortunate thing is that a kg of water only stores 9.8J per meter, enough to run a 10W LED for about 1 second.

author: @technocracynow9339
text: I got kicked out of my House today, but your laugh got me the first smile of the day, Thanks for that, Thanks for science!

replies.author: @technocracynow9339
replies.text:
@kilokilosI used an Hoe instead of  an Lawn mower and used local "Weeds" instead of Tropical Plants in the Garden, so the Landlord/Lessor (sorry don't know whats the right word) mowed everything down, he bought a lock so i can't get in there anymore. Then I Was upset and screamed at him why he even matter, when someone took care of the Garden. I had to hurry because i had to pick up my child from the kindergarden, but when i Was back at Home i found the termination notice in the Mailbox.. 
I'm from Germany and things like this are Happening everyday we even call that "The most German Thing ever" 2mm dried out Lawn, boxwood and Junipers and if someone don't mow their lawn atleast 3 Times a day then we simply just start another war...

replies.author: @stevetobias4890
replies.text: @technocracynow9339 sorry to hear this story, you need to move here in Australia, this wouldn't happen because the person renting has rights and must be given at least 30 days notice of a termination of the renting contract. Also once a resident you have many organisations that step in to ensure that you and your child have a place to call home

replies.author: @kilokilos
replies.text: @technocracynow9339 very German that, I am German 10 generations back, we had to leave the place because we would not change the religion. Unfortunately the same people are now back that killed 25% of us in 1902 to "start another war"

author: @qvivnb
text: The biggest issue is the 'weakness' of gravity because even if you dropped a 100kg mass from a height of 10 meters you will generate less than 10,000J of energy (<3Wh).

author: @ArcanusLibero
text: A great security system. Put the light where it will shine on an intruders eyes and the weight hits him on the head. Nice project. A guy I met in the desert had a solar array that moved water to his tower tank. The water would flow all night down into his reservoir tank. The water was used to spin in line turbines and create power for his home in the evening. A kind of mechanical gravity battery if you will.
